items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Clonmines
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* irelands ancient east
*County:* County Wexford
*Type:* monastery
*Period:* 13th-16th century
*Coords:* 52.261013, -6.765701
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clonmines
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Clonmines/@52.268,-6.838,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/82/Clonmines_East_View_2010_09_27.jpg/330px-Clonmines_East_View_2010_09_27.jpg
*Gallery:* 2 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Clonmines is a civil parish and townland in the Bannow Bay area of County Wexford, Ireland, the site of "the finest example in Ireland of a deserted medieval borough". It is situated in the barony of Shelburne, southwest of Wellingtonbridge on the northwest shore of Bannow Bay. The parish of Clonmines contains the townland of the same name and the smaller townland of Arklow, with respective areas of 1,258 acres (509 ha) and 127 acres (51 ha).

_Listing 2 of 212_

listing.desc: Clonmines is a civil parish and townland in the Bannow Bay area of County Wexford, Ireland, the site of "the finest example in Ireland of a deserted medieval borough". The parish of Clonmines contains the townland of the same name and the smaller townland of Arklow, with respective areas of 1,258 acres (509 ha) and 127 acres (51 ha).

imageAttribution.description: Ruins of the medieval town Clonmines, as seen from the Bannow bay.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Kanturk Castle
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Cork
*Type:* fortified house
*Period:* 17th century
*Coords:* 52.175, -8.9
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kanturk
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Kanturk%20Castle/@52.1778,-8.9056,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Kanturk%2C_County_Cork_-_Kanturk_Castle.jpg/330px-Kanturk%2C_County_Cork_-_Kanturk_Castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 8 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Kanturk is a town in the north west of County Cork, Ireland. It is situated at the confluence of the Allua (Allow) and Dallow (Dalua) rivers, which stream further on as tributaries to the River Blackwater. It is about 50 kilometres from Cork and Limerick, and lies just north of the main N72 road, 15 km from Mallow and about 40 km from Killarney. Kanturk is within the Cork North-West Dáil constituency.

_Listing 4 of 212_

listing.name: Kanturk Castle
listing.desc: Kanturk is a town in the north west of County Cork, Ireland. It is situated at the confluence of the Allua (Allow) and Dallow (Dalua) rivers, which stream further on as tributaries to the River Blackwater. It is about 50 kilometres from Cork and Limerick, and lies just north of the main N72 road, 15 km from Mallow and about 40 km from Killarney. Kanturk is within the Cork North-West Dáil constituency.

*Name:* Castle Matrix
*Country:* ireland
*Region:* kingdom of munster
*County:* County Limerick
*Type:* tower house
*Period:* 15th century
*Coords:* 54.03972222, 19.02777778
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Malbork_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Castle%20Matrix/@52.45,-8.928,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/castle-matrix.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* no
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* looks like this is in poland? 

*Description:*
The Castle of the Teutonic Order in Malbork, commonly known as Malbork Castle, is a Brick Gothic castle complex located in the town of Malbork, Poland, built in the 13th and significantly expanded in the 14th century. It is the largest castle in the world measured by land area and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

_Listing 5 of 212_
